# Pranav Gore

> Senior Backend Engineer, 12+ years. Founding/early engineer at Avoma and Privyr. Python, Django, AWS, PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, ClickHouse, FFmpeg/HLS, Terraform, RAG pipelines.

## Who I am

I am a senior backend software engineer based in Singapore with over 12 years of experience building production-grade backend systems. I have been a founding or early engineer at two startups: Avoma (AI meeting intelligence platform) and Privyr (mobile CRM for sales professionals). My career spans early-stage greenfield work, scaling distributed systems, and full infrastructure ownership at startups with global user bases.

I am currently the Backend Tech Lead at Privyr, where I own the entire backend and infrastructure: every API, every database decision, every deployment, every incident.

## Current Work (Privyr, 2019 - present)

- Backend Tech Lead owning the full backend and infrastructure of a mobile CRM platform serving sales professionals across Southeast Asia
- Built the system from scratch; it now processes 200,000 leads per day at 3,000 to 4,000 requests per minute
- Shipped a production RAG pipeline on WhatsApp using Amazon Bedrock and ClickHouse for semantic retrieval
- Built webhook infrastructure integrating 15+ platforms including Facebook Lead Ads, Google Ads, and Zapier
- Integrated Gemini AI for unstructured email parsing
- Ran live zero-downtime database migrations across 300+ million records
- Resolved a live DDoS attack and debugged a VPC IP exhaustion outage during peak hours

## Previous Work

**Avoma (2016 - 2019), Founding Engineer**
- Joined when the engineering team was 1 person; helped scale it to 22 engineers
- Built the video processing pipeline: each meeting spins up a dedicated EC2 instance, records, uploads to S3, terminates automatically
- Pipeline backed by Kafka and Faust for event-driven processing
- Platform records 1,500 hours of video meetings daily

**Red Hat (2014 - 2016), Software Engineer**
- Built core APIs in Go for OpenShift.io
- Built a real-time multiplayer gaming platform on Node.js and Redis
- Built a mobile SDK that was acquired by Marketo

## Technical Skills

**Languages:** Python, Go, JavaScript

**Frameworks:** Django, Django REST Framework

**Databases:** PostgreSQL, Redis, ClickHouse, MySQL

**Message Queues:** Kafka, RabbitMQ, Faust

**Cloud and Infrastructure:** AWS (EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, Bedrock, VPC, IAM), Terraform, CloudFormation, Docker

**Observability:** Grafana, Prometheus

**Video:** FFmpeg, HLS, adaptive bitrate streaming

**AI/ML:** RAG pipelines, Amazon Bedrock, Gemini AI, LLM integration

**CI/CD:** GitHub Actions, Jenkins

## Notable Technical Achievements

- Designed and shipped a production RAG pipeline on WhatsApp at scale
- Resolved a live DDoS attack in production with zero customer data loss
- Ran a zero-downtime migration across 300+ million live database records
- Debugged a VPC IP exhaustion outage during peak hours
- Scaled a video meeting platform to 1,500 hours of recorded meetings daily
- Built a lead distribution system handling 200,000 leads per day

## Writing (Technical Blog)

- [Putting an LLM in Production: Structure, Cost, and Prompt-Injection Defenses](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/llm-in-production-structure-cost-defenses/) - Wrapping an untrusted LLM inside code that refuses to trust it: one job per call, completion computed from confidence scores the model can't fake, a reliability sandwich, per-conversation cost tracking, and layered prompt-injection defenses
- [Conversational AI Agents: Reply to Conversations, Not Messages](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/conversational-ai-agents-reply-to-conversations/) - Why a WhatsApp lead-qualification agent attaches to a conversation and polls its state instead of replying per message: burst debouncing, the poll loop, silence handling, and treating every exit condition as a designed product outcome
- [We Didn't Need Temporal: Durable Agent Runs on Postgres and Celery](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/durable-agent-runs-postgres-celery/) - How we built durable, crash-safe, long-running conversational agents on a single Postgres table and Celery, and why durable-execution requirements are separable from the orchestration features workflow engines bundle with them
- [Diagnosing PostgreSQL Connection Leaks on RDS](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/rds-connection-leak/) - How a Gunicorn and PostgreSQL statement_timeout mismatch caused orphaned RDS connections and 502 errors under load, diagnosed using pg_stat_activity
- [Under Fire: What a Real DDoS Attack Looks Like and How We Fought Back](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/ddos-at-privyr/) - A real-time account of surviving a 30-hour DDoS attack at Privyr and the product change shipped right after
- [RDS PostgreSQL 13 to 15 Upgrade with GCP DataStream](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/rds-postgres-13-to-15-upgrade/) - Checklist and gotchas for upgrading RDS PostgreSQL 13 to 15 with logical replication and GCP DataStream in the picture
- [When & why to use PyAutoGUI](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/pyautogui/) - Practical use cases, limitations, and a getting-started walkthrough for desktop GUI automation and testing
- [Writing custom VSCode plugin](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/vscode_plugin/) - Building a custom Visual Studio Code extension from scaffolding to shipping a working developer utility
- [X virtual framebuffer (Xvfb)](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/Xvfb/) - What Xvfb is and how to use it to run GUI applications in headless Linux environments for CI/CD and testing
- [Video File Operations with FFmpeg](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/ffmpeg_operations/) - Common FFmpeg operations: trimming, merging, format conversion, resolution scaling, and extracting audio
- [Prevent accidental git-push in a simple step](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/gitpush/) - A one-line git configuration trick to prevent accidentally pushing to the wrong upstream repository
- [Python code translator 2 to 3](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/2to3/) - Using Python's built-in 2to3 tool to automatically translate a Python 2 codebase to Python 3 syntax
- [Record Screen and Audio using FFmpeg on macOS](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/ffmpeg_mac/) - Recording screen and system audio on macOS using FFmpeg from the command line, no third-party software
- [Exported but Restricted inside `internal` package](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/golang_imported/) - How Go's internal package mechanism restricts exported symbols to specific import paths
- [Update golang version on Fedora](https://pranavgore.com/blogs/golang_update/) - Safely updating a Go installation on Fedora Linux while keeping the old version as a fallback

Blog: https://pranavgore.com/blogs/
